rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Probate Orders
rulings.Rulings: Objector's Motion to Set Aside Probate Orders is denied. Procedurally, the Motion is untimely under CCP §473, particularly since the Objector appeared in Court in April of 2009, within the window of time prescribed by CCP §473. Substantively, the Motion cannot be brought under either Probate Code § 8007 or § 8270 under the current facts and law of the case, as it is the law of the case that Objector lacks standing in this probate matter, as he was disinherited by the language of Decedent's Will. The Court makes no determination in this Order regarding Objector's extrinsic fraud allegations, nor will this Court exceed its jurisdiction to make any such determination while the issue is pending before any other civil or appellate court. Petitioner's request for sanctions is denied, for not being in compliance with CCP §128.7. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, April 5, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Limited Objection To Referee'S Recommendation Re: Motion #208
rulings.Rulings: Motion No. 208 is returned to Referee Sabraw to determine in the first instance whether Respondent is entitled to any additional relief as to some or all of document numbers 45, 46, 47, 72, 79, 80, 82, 84, 86, 87, and 96 given her ruling on the substance of the motion. In making that determination, she is free to consider the manner in which Motion No. 208 was presented and argued by the parties. In light of this ruling, the Court will not hold a hearing on the objection (without prejudice to renewing an objection, if appropriate, after Judge Sabraw's further determination).
